commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Old. Houbigant would translate, "when he was growing old "senesce ret, as he supposes he was now nearly sixty, having judged about twenty-five years, and living another twenty as partner with Saul. (Prol. lxii.) See chap. vii. 15. (Haydock)
Judges, as his delegates in the southern parts of the country. (Calmet)
Josephus says one of them was stationed at Bethel. (Antiquities vi. 3.)
